notes:
VERIFIED. Plasma Blast (bw10) is a standard BW-era set: 10 modeled cards/pack, 36-pack booster box AND 8-pack ETB both confirmed real products (ToyWiz/PokeBeach: ETB = 8 packs + 40 energy + accessories). shipEv=true.

PRIMARY SOURCE = ThePriceDex per-set pull-rate table for bw10 (fetched verbatim): Secret Rare 1 in 112.5; Ultra Rare (Full Art) 1 in 35.5; Rare Holo EX 1 in 18; Rare ACE 1 in 24; Rare Holo 1 in 4.2; Rare 1 in 1.5. Per-box figures match: ~2 EX/box, ~1.0 UR/box, ~0.3 SR/box. Sample size undisclosed -> confidence "medium" (not "high").

CROSS-CHECK vs Flipside Gaming BW-era baseline (fetched): Pokemon-EX ~1:18 packs (EXACT match to ThePriceDex 1:18); Full Art UR ~1:18-36 (ThePriceDex 1:35.5 sits at the lean end, consistent); Secret Rare ~1:72 baseline (ThePriceDex 1:112.5 leaner — Plasma Blast has only 4 secrets, so a leaner rate is expected, within set variance). Order of magnitude corroborated by two independent sources.

BUCKET MAPPING — keys are a strict subset of this set's buckets {common:26, rare:19, uncommon:33, ex:6, rare-holo:11, ultra-rare:6, secret-rare:4}. Confirmed against pokemontcg.io raw rarities (105 cards): Common 26, Uncommon 33, Rare 15, Rare ACE 4, Rare Holo 11, Rare Holo EX 6, Rare Ultra 6, Rare Secret 4. CRITICAL: in this set's bucketing the 4 ACE SPEC cards (G Booster, G Scope, Master Ball + Computer Search/Scramble Switch) are folded into the `rare` bucket (15 Rare + 4 ACE = 19), NOT ultra-rare — there is no separate ACE bucket. So I fold ThePriceDex's "Rare ACE 1:24" into the `rare` bucket, NOT ultra-rare. The `ex` bucket = Rare Holo EX (1:18); `ultra-rare` = Full Art (Rare Ultra, 1:35.5); `secret-rare` = Rare Secret (1:112.5).

RARE-OR-BETTER SLOT (fires every pack; conditional dist = per-pack rates):
- secret-rare = 1/112.5 = 0.0089
- ultra-rare  = 1/35.5  = 0.0282
- ex          = 1/18    = 0.0556
- rare-holo   = 1/4.2   = 0.2381
- rare        = plain rare (0.6276) + Rare ACE (1/24 = 0.0417) = 0.6692
Sum = 1.0000 exactly (rounding residual absorbed into `rare`).

REVERSE-HOLO SLOT (separate parallel slot): ThePriceDex Reverse Rare Holo 1/11.2 = 0.0893, Reverse Rare 1/7.2 = 0.1389; remainder split common/uncommon by pool counts (26 common, 33 uncommon) -> common 0.3401, uncommon 0.4317. Sum = 1.0.

Pack = 5 common + 3 uncommon + 1 reverse-holo + 1 rare-or-better = 10. No shiny-rare or rainbow bucket in BW era — correctly absent. Vintage 2013 sealed product carries a collector premium, so per-pack EV will sit well below current sealed box prices — expected, rates NOT inflated.
